headline: AMC Veteran David Zabel Lands Netflix Cop Drama in Strategic Studio Deal
lede: Fresh off his successful run with The Walking Dead franchise, showrunner David Zabel has secured a significant overall deal with AMC Studios and positioned a gritty new series at Netflix.
body:
David Zabel's trajectory in television storytelling continues its upward arc, with the veteran producer leveraging his zombie apocalypse credentials into a broader development strategy.

The multi-year overall deal with AMC Studios signals the network's confidence in Zabel's creative vision, particularly after his successful management of The Walking Dead: Daryl Dixon through multiple seasons. By simultaneously setting up a new cop drama at Netflix, Zabel demonstrates strategic professional mobility in an increasingly competitive streaming landscape.

Zabel's proven track record of transforming genre narratives positions him as a sought-after creative executive. His ability to navigate complex franchise storytelling with The Walking Dead franchise suggests the upcoming Netflix project will likely feature nuanced character dynamics and intense procedural elements.

The new cop drama remains untitled, with plot specifics currently under wraps. Industry observers will be watching closely to see how Zabel translates his apocalyptic storytelling skills into a contemporary law enforcement narrative.
key_facts: Multi-year overall deal with AMC Studios
Cop drama set up at Netflix
Previous showrunner for The Walking Dead: Daryl Dixon
